description: A Slot 2 particle announcing that the next Phi token is a proper designation. The token may be a content word borne as a name or a productive onym with no dictionary entry. The marker changes how the listener receives the form; it assigns no meaning or kind to the bearer.
articulatory_notes: For 'n', the tongue rests against the upper teeth while voice resonates through the nose. The contact releases into the mid-front vowel 'e', all within one stressed syllable.
usage_notes: The particle comes before any honorific and then the name atom. Published and machine-validated Phi keeps 'ne' at every mention; established conversation may omit it while the referent remains clear. A productive onym has two, three, or four legal Phi syllables, while a source name outside that shape remains outside the Phi passage unless its bearer accepts an adaptation.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The listener hears before the name arrives that the token identifies rather than describes. This lets a chosen designation remain distinct from any ordinary lexical meaning.
